== Notes for [[Item:Q2568|Q2568]] ==

<p style="text-align: left;" dir="ltr">&nbsp;</p>
<p><p style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; margin: 0.5em 0px 1em; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">Traditionally, Samoyedic languages and peoples have been divided into two major areal groups: Northern Samoyedic (Nenets, Yurats, Enets, Nganasans), and Southern Samoyedic (Selkups) with a further now-extinct subgroup of Sayan-Samoyedics (Kamasins, Mators) named after the<span>&nbsp;</span><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" title="Sayan Mountains" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sayan_Mountains">Sayan Mountains</a>. They are however purely geographical, and do not reflect linguistic relations.</p>
<p><p style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; margin: 0.5em 0px 1em; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">Linguistic genealogical classifications point to an early divergence of Nganasan and (perhaps to a lesser degree) Mator, with Enets&ndash;Nenets&ndash;Yurats and Kamas&ndash;Selkup forming internal branches.<sup id="cite_ref-Janhunen1998_4-1" class="reference" style="font-size: 12px; white-space: nowrap; font-weight: normal; font-style: normal; line-height: 1;"><a style="text-decoration: none; background: none transparent scroll repeat 0% 0%; border-radius: 0px; overflow-wrap: break-word;" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samoyedic_languages#cite_note-Janhunen1998-4"><span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" style="pointer-events: none;">]</span></a></sup></p>
<p><div class="treeview" style="font-size: 16px; font-family: sans-serif; white-space: normal; word-spacing: 0px; text-transform: none; font-weight: 400; color: #202122; font-style: normal; orphans: 2; widows: 2; letter-spacing: normal; background-color: #ffffff; text-indent: 0px; font-variant-ligatures: normal; font-variant-caps: normal; -webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; text-decoration-thickness: initial; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;">&nbsp;</div>

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
671BCEEF1422F1EFBC119822EF79916C41E9


[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
